label: Token sync
headline: Figma variables ↔ Git
lead: Two-way sync between Figma variables and a token repo, with a three-way diff before anything lands.
asideIntro: An anonymized Figma plugin panel for pulling design tokens from a Git repo into Figma variables, and pushing edits back as a pull request.
heroAlt: The token sync plugin open in Figma on a laptop, browsing colour variable collections with the pull and push panel.
aside: Configure provider, repository, and credentials in settings
Pull Tokens Studio JSON into collections, modes, and styles
Push Figma edits back to the repo with a clean diff
Typography and shadow composites map to native Figma styles

modal.title: Setup
modal.body: Install the plugin from manifest.json in the repo. Connect your Git provider (GitHub or Azure DevOps), repository, and personal access token in the settings tab.

modal.title: Pull: repo → Figma
modal.body:
• Reads Tokens Studio JSON from the configured branch
  • Creates or maps Figma variable collections and modes via $themes.json
  • Imports typography and box-shadow tokens as native text and effect styles

modal.title: Push: Figma → repo
modal.body:
• Exports variable and style changes back to token JSON
  • Opens a pull request instead of writing directly to main
  • Three-way diff compares repo, Figma, and conflicts before apply

modal.title: This page
modal.body: This page shows the plugin in a device mockup. Variable binding, Git fetch, and PR creation run inside Figma: install it from the repo to use it on a real file.
